question: Does a same-workgroup token-tile pairwise segmented epilogue improve the CK direct-fragment compressed dKV producer on the actual S4096 LightningIndexer route?

implementation.producer: CK Tile BF16 M16N16K32 direct-fragment dKV contribution producer
implementation.epilogue: same-CTA LDS page over token_block x K16 x D16, pairwise leader scan by compressed group, reduced global atomicAdd
implementation.read: This diagnostic still consumes materialized probs and scaled grad logits. It isolates whether the local segmented epilogue is worth lifting into the no-HSK source-boundary sparse-MLA backward owner.

correctness.read: The reduced epilogue matches the raw atomic control closely enough for this timing gate; the rejection is performance, not math.

route_model.read: The write-reduction opportunity exists, but this pairwise leader-scan implementation serializes too much epilogue work per token tile and is far slower than raw atomics.

decision.keep: CK direct-fragment raw atomic dKV producer remains the best isolated dKV slice to lift into the source-boundary rewrite.
decision.reject: Naive same-workgroup pairwise segmented reduction over token_block x K16 entries.
decision.next_sparse_mla_kernel_step: Move CK direct-fragment contribution math into the source-boundary recompute owner and focus on avoiding repeated QK/dO.V score recompute. If local reduction is revisited, it needs a warp/block segmented primitive or compact active-slot table with O(1) slot lookup, not per-entry leader scans.
